title: National Health Accounts (NHA) Indicators — Saudi Arabia
abstract: Health financing and expenditure indicators for Saudi Arabia (e.g. Current Health Expenditure as % of GDP, per-capita health expenditure, expenditure by financing scheme, health care function, and provider type), reported annually from 2000 onward, following the WHO/OECD/Eurostat System of Health Accounts framework.

resources.name: NHA indicators, Saudi Arabia, 2000-2018+ (XLSX)
resources.path: KAUST - DATA/Human Nutrition and Health/NHA indicators.xlsx

variables.name: indicator
variables.label: NHA Indicator
variables.description: National Health Accounts indicator name (e.g. Current Health Expenditure (CHE) as % GDP, Compulsory Health Insurance as % CHE, expenditure by health care function/provider/financing scheme).

variables.name: value_by_year
variables.label: Indicator Value by Year
variables.unit: varies (% of GDP, US$, US$ per capita)
variables.description: Annual indicator value in wide format, one column per year from 2000 onward.

steward.name: KAUST Human Nutrition and Health Data Team

access.how_to_request: Publicly available from the WHO Global Health Expenditure Database (apps.who.int/nha/database); no request required.

provenance.originator: World Health Organization (WHO) Global Health Expenditure Database, National Health Accounts — Saudi Arabia extract
provenance.citation: World Health Organization. Global Health Expenditure Database. Geneva: WHO.

known_issues: File contains only Saudi Arabia (single-country extract); exact extraction/download date and WHO release vintage not recorded in the file itself, could not be independently confirmed against the live WHO database without internet access at cataloging time.
Health financing/expenditure indicators are not nutrition-specific; included for national health-system context per the Human Nutrition and Health domain scope.
No sub-national spatial detail; applied uniformly across all five subbasins as national-level statistics.
